MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-24-2018 Continued To Jul-08-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Jun-27-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Edward O.C. Ord of Ord & Newman, Tel. No. 1-415-274-3800 appearing for
the Plaintiffs;
Atty. Lucy F. Wang, Deputy Atty. General, Tel. No. 1-415-510-3543 appearing
for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiffs Edward and Evelyn Silva seek summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication on their complaint for a tax refund. Plaintiffs assert that defendant California Franchise Tax Board did not mail them a notice of proposed assessment (NPA) for a purported 2009 tax deficiency and, even if it did, the NPA was mailed to the wrong address, voiding the assessment. In February 2014, an FTB tax auditor notified the Silvas' tax representative, CPA John Lavorato, that the FTB's audit of the Silvas' 2009 taxes was being closed and a NPA would issue within 90 days, after which the Silvas would have 60 days to file a protest. (Li Dec. 7 & Ex. E.) The Silvas assert that the FTB "never issued" a NPA and, if it did, the NPA "was never mailed." (RUMF 7.) The FTB disputes these allegations, citing documentation that includes a certificate of mailing the NPA. (RUMF 7, 9, 22) These are triable issues of material fact that preclude summary adjudication. (See CCP 437c.)
The summary judgment record is also rife with other triable factual disputes. For example: (1) did the Silvas intend to "mandate the FTB to send all notices and communications" to Lavorato? (2) what did the FTB's communications with Lavorato include? (3) was the FTB purporting to "compl[y] with Form 3520"? (4) did the Silvas receive a copy of the NPA before October 24, 2014? (5) when was the FTB's last communication with Lavorato? (6) was a regular procedure followed for collecting the Silvas' mail, and, if so, what was it? (RUMF 4, 5, 6, 9, 10, 11, 12.) The issues of law raised by the summary judgment papers may also be addressed at trial. Plaintiffs' motion is DENIED. Order signed. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Robert Balian, CSR #5220 =302/ru
